data.title: Germany’s manufacturing, services, and composite PMIs rise in October
data.description:
• The HCOB flash Germany composite PMI output index rose from 52.0 in September to 53.8 in October, signaling a further acceleration in the pace of growth to the quickest since May 2023.
  • Manufacturing PMI in Germany rose to a 2-month high of 49.60 points in October from 49.50 points in September.
  • Services PMI in Germany increased to 54.50 points in October from 51.50 points in September.
  • Growth of total activity was supported by a renewed increase in new business volumes in October, following slight declines in both August and September. 
  • German private sector companies remained confident of growth in activity over the next 12 months. 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Business activity in France falls at quickest pace since February
data.description:
• The headline HCOB Flash France Composite PMI Output Index fell for a second month in a row in October to 46.8, from 48.1 in September.
  • Manufacturing PMI increased to 48.30 points in October from 48.20 points in September.
  • Services PMI decreased to 47.10 points in October from 48.50 points in September.
  • The figures pointed to a downturn in private sector business activity that was the sharpest for eight months.
  • “The subdued trend in France’s private sector persists. The Flash Composite PMI for October fell to 46.8, indicating a continued and stronger contraction in overall economic activity. Output in both manufacturing and services is declining, pointing to broad-based weakness. Our in-house HCOB nowcasting model predicts zero growth for the third quarter." said Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Xiaomi says rising chip costs have pushed up phone prices after Redmi K90 launch disappoints market
data.description:
• Chinese smartphone maker Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY) said on Friday that increasing memory chip prices have raised handset production costs, following market disappointment over the pricing of its new Redmi K90 series.
  • "Cost pressure has transferred to the pricing of our new products," President Lu Weibing wrote on Weibo, as per Reuters. "Rising costs of memory chips are far beyond expectations and could intensify."
  • Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) (OTCPK:XIACY) launched its entry-level Redmi K90 on Thursday with the base model, equipped with 12 GB of memory and 256 GB of storage, priced from 2,599 yuan ($364). That compares with 2,499 yuan for the base model of the predecessor K80 series launched in November 2024.
  • Lu noted consumer disappointment about price gaps between configurations. He said Xiaomi will lower the price of the most in-demand K90 model - with 12 GB memory and 512 GB storage - by 300 yuan to 2,899 yuan for the first month of sales, the report said.

data.title: Spain's unemployment rate rises more than expected in Q3
data.description:
• The unemployment rate in Spain increased to 10.45% in Q3 from 10.29% in Q2, higher than the estimated 10.20%.

  • The producer prices increased 0.30% in September over the same month in the previous year.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

data.title: NatWest reports Q3 results
data.description:
• NatWest press release (NWG): Q3 Total income of £326M (+14.8% Y/Y).
  • Total assets and liabilities increased by £6.1 billion and £6.2 billion to £189.3 billion and £182.4 billion respectively at 30 September 2025, compared with 31 December 2024.
  • NWM Plc's leverage ratio decreased to 4.5% at 30 September 2025 compared with 5.5% at 31 December 2024, driven by the decrease in Tier 1 capital and higher leverage exposure reflecting increases in trading assets and other financial assets.

data.title: Eni raises 2025 share buy-back by €0.3B to €1.8B
data.description:
• Eni (NYSE:E) on Friday raised its 2025 share buyback commitment by €0.3 billion to €1.8 billion, supported by strategic progress and an improved FY'25 CFFO outlook.
  • Eni increased the group’s expected CFFO before working capital adjustments to €12 billion from the previous €11.5 billion.

data.title: Blackstone to invest $705M in India’s Federal Bank, becoming its largest shareholder
data.description:
• Blackstone (NYSE:BX) will invest about $705 million in India's Federal Bank for a 9.9% stake, the private lender said on Friday.
  • The deal will make the private equity firm the largest shareholder in the bank.

data.title: Disney warns YouTube TV could lose ESPN, National Geographic, FX and other channels by month-end
data.description:
Disney (NYSE:DIS) warned that ESPN, ABC, and other major networks could be pulled from YouTube TV by the end of the month if a new agreement is not reached by October 30 11:59 p.m. ET.

The ongoing dispute over new carriage fees, with Disney (NYSE:DIS) demanding higher payments, puts nearly 10 million YouTube TV subscribers at risk of losing access to key Disney-owned networks, including local ABC stations, all ESPN channels, FX, National Geographic, Disney Channel, Disney Jr., Freeform, and more. Disney (NYSE:DIS) started notifying YouTube TV customers of a potential blackout. 

Google's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) YouTube TV cited concerns about increased costs for its customers. “Unfortunately, Disney is proposing costly economic terms that would raise prices on YouTube TV customers and give our customers fewer choices.” a YouTube TV spokesperson said in an official statement.

YouTube TV subscribers pay $72.99 per month currently. Major price hikes follow blackouts because companies negotiate from stronger positions. YouTube TV said it would offer subscribers a $20 credit if Disney’s (DIS) networks remain unavailable for an extended period.

data.title: Chandra Asri to buy Exxon's Singapore retail fuel station network Esso
data.description:
Chandra Asri Pacific has signed a sale and purchase Agreement to acquire ExxonMobil's (NYSE:XOM) Esso-branded retail fuel station network in Singapore through a special purpose vehicle under a wholly-owned subsidiary of the group, the company said on Friday.

Chandra Asri Group will continue to use the Esso brand, continue purchasing branded fuel from ExxonMobil, and maintain all existing customer loyalty points and cards.
